helpful: Provisioned questions-presented, petition, and BIO texts were excellent — the union BIO alone surfaced the decisive signal (cert denied in Macy's, No. 25-627, on the same Thryv question two days before the BIO was filed)
statpack relist/CVSG/circuit cuts gave clean anchors
gaps: The federal respondent's (SG's) brief in opposition was on the docket but not provisioned as text; the SG's framing is often the strongest cert signal in cases where the government is respondent

fedcourts query returned no priors for NLRB/labor citations — a topic or QP-text similarity search over resolved SCOTUS cert petitions would be more useful than citation-overlap matching for petitions
No salience-band table in this statpack, though the prompt describes one; the per-fee-class (paid vs IFP) grant rates were also not in the markdown, only referenced as living in statpack.json
notes: Two corpus query calls each cost ~110 MB of ranged reads and returned nothing; cheap misses would be nice (e.g. a count-only probe).
